name: Zerodha Order Book Manager
scenario: Zerodha's order book stores live trade orders as a linked list. Build operations for insertion, deletion, and queries with O(1) operations at the head.

core.description: OrderBook (LL): addHead(Order), addTail(Order), delete(orderId), get(index): O(n). Use dummy head. Test: add 3, delete middle, verify remaining.
removeNthFromEnd(head,n): dummy+two-pointer, one pass O(n) O(1). Test: [1,2,3,4,5] n=2→[1,2,3,5].
mergeTwoSortedOrders(l1,l2): O(n+m) O(1) dummy head. Test: [1,3,5]+[2,4,6]→[1,2,3,4,5,6].
@SpringBootTest (4 tests): removeNth correct, merge sorted correct, addTwo=807, deleteDups correct.
README: explains dummy head pattern and why it eliminates edge cases.

bonus.description: copyRandomList(head): O(n) O(1) interleave method. Verify random pointers deep-copied.

ai_policy_explanation: 5 hints. Dummy head: ListNode dummy=new ListNode(0); dummy.next=head; — prev never null. removeNthFromEnd: fast advances n+1 steps (not n!) before both advance together. Gap n+1 ensures slow.next is the target. mergeTwoSorted: while(l1!=null&&l2!=null) compare and append; after loop curr.next = whichever list still has nodes. addTwo: while(l1!=null||l2!=null||carry!=0) — three conditions. carry=sum/10; digit=sum%10. Delete duplicates: if curr.val==curr.next.val: curr.next=curr.next.next (skip); else curr=curr.next (advance). Never advance on skip.
